label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So the problem is about Jane's birthday and calculating a date a month ago from today. Let me go through the details step by step. First, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Wait, February's last day is usually the 28th, except in a leap year when it's the 29th. But 2001 isn't a leap year, right? Leap years are every 4 years, like 2000, 2004, 2008, etc. So 2001 isn't a leap year, so her birthday would be February 28, 2001. Now, today is her 16th birthday. So if she was born in 2001, her 16th birthday would be in 2001 + 16 = 2017. So today's date is February 28, 2017. Wait, but is 2017 a leap year? Let me check. 2016 was a leap year, then the next one is 2020. So 2017 isn't a leap year, so February only has 28 days. So her birthday is on February 28, 2017. The question is asking for the date a month ago from today. So if today is February 28, 2017, then a month ago would be January 28, 2017. Because if you subtract one month from February 28, you get January 28. Wait, but sometimes when people say a month ago, they might mean 30 days ago or subtracting a calendar month. But since we're dealing with dates, it's more likely to be subtracting one month from the current date. Looking at the options, (D) is 01/28/2017.
